---
title: "glycoMusubi: Vignettes & Technical Guide"
author: "Yusuke Matsui"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
site: bookdown::bookdown_site
documentclass: book
bibliography: references.bib
biblio-style: apalike
link-citations: yes
description: "Comprehensive technical guide for the glycoMusubi knowledge graph, embedding models, and hierarchical glycan prediction benchmark."
---

# Preface {-}

[日本語版はこちら](../book-vignette-ja/_book/index.html){target="_blank"}

This book provides a comprehensive guide to **glycoMusubi**, an integrated knowledge graph for glycobiology research. It covers the biological foundations, computational methods, and practical workflows for using glycoMusubi in research.

## Who is this book for? {-}

- **Glycobiologists** seeking computational tools for glycan prediction
- **Bioinformaticians** building knowledge graph pipelines
- **Machine learning researchers** working on biomedical KG embeddings
- **Students** learning about glycosylation and knowledge graphs

## Book structure {-}

| Part | Chapters | Content |
|------|----------|---------|
| **Foundations** | 1--2 | Glycobiology primer and KG concepts |
| **Architecture** | 3--5 | glycoMusubi design, ETL pipeline, and embedding models |
| **Benchmarks** | 6--7 | Hierarchical prediction and cell-type analysis |
| **Practice** | 8--9 | R workflows, use cases, and applications |

## How to cite {-}

> Matsui Y. (2026) Hierarchical glycan prediction from protein sequence reveals a determination boundary at the structural-class level. *Bioinformatics*.

## Source code {-}

All code is available at <https://github.com/ymatts/glycoMusubi>.
